# Pandoc — Universal Document Converter

## Metadata
- Author: John MacFarlane

## Summary
Pandoc is a free and open-source document converter written in Haskell by John MacFarlane. It converts files between markup formats such as Markdown, reStructuredText, HTML, LaTeX, and Microsoft Word docx. Pandoc was first released in 2006. It supports exporting to PDF via a LaTeX engine. The project is distributed under the GPL license.

This source is referenced by the following entity pages:
- [[Pandoc — Universal Document Converter]]
===END FILE===

# Pandoc

## Overview
Pandoc is a free and open-source document converter written in Haskell by John MacFarlane.

## Key Facts
- Converts files between markup formats such as Markdown, reStructuredText, HTML, LaTeX, and Microsoft Word docx.
- First released in 2006.
- Supports exporting to PDF via a LaTeX engine.
- Distributed under the GPL license.

## Related
None.
